turns.user_message: I noticed my chest gets tight whenever I hear a sudden loud noise, which is weird because it never used to bother me at all. I just sit there waiting for the next thing to happen, feeling completely on edge for no real reason. My hands start sweating and I have to consciously remind myself to breathe normally. It feels like my body is stuck in this weird alert mode over absolutely nothing. I try to shake it off, but the tension just lingers in my shoulders all day.

This constant state of readiness has completely taken over my routine, and I keep wondering why my nerves are so frayed all the time now. I catch myself jumping at the smallest sounds, like a door closing down the hall or a phone vibrating on the table. My stomach knots up before I even know what triggered it, leaving me exhausted from just trying to stay calm. It used to be so easy to brush things off, but now every little shift in the room feels like a warning sign. I am just so tired of feeling like I am waiting for the other shoe to drop.

I feel completely trapped inside my own skin because my body refuses to stand down even when everything around me is perfectly quiet. Every single breath feels heavy and shallow, like my lungs are fighting against an invisible weight that just will not lift. I pace back and forth trying to burn off this frantic energy, but my heart keeps hammering against my ribs like a trapped bird. It is exhausting to live like this when I know there is absolutely nothing to be afraid of right now. I just want my nervous system to finally remember how to relax and let me be still.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with something they noticed about themselves. This used to not bother the speaker.
